name: import-guide
title: MicroPython Import Rules & Common Mistakes
description: How MicroPython resolves imports and the most common AI mistakes when writing MicroPython code.

rules.title: NEVER use 'u' prefix module names — use standard Python names
rules.explanation: Old MicroPython used 'ujson', 'urequests', 'utime', 'urandom', etc. These are DEPRECATED. Modern MicroPython (1.24+) maps standard names: 'json', 'requests', 'time', 'random'. Always use the standard Python name. The 'u' prefix is legacy and should never appear in new code.
required: NOTE: On some custom-compiled ESP32 firmware builds, 'requests', 'urequests', 'umqtt', 'websocket' and other modules are frozen into the firmware and available without mip install. On standard MicroPython builds, install requests via: import mip; mip.install('requests')
from machine import Pin, ADC, I2C, SPI, PWM
rules.zero_tolerance: If ANY 'u' prefix appears in generated code — whether as 'import ujson' or 'from ujson import ...' — it is WRONG. Reject it immediately. Also rejects any code referencing 'u' modules via sys.modules or __import__().

rules.title: lib/ directory files DO NOT use 'lib.' prefix
rules.explanation: Files in /lib/ (or /flash/lib/) are added to sys.path directly. The directory name 'lib' is NOT a Python package namespace.
rules.wrong:
import lib.mymodule
from lib.mymodule import MyClass
import lib.ssd1306
import lib.requests
rules.correct:
import mymodule
from mymodule import MyClass
import ssd1306
import requests
rules.note: AI often writes 'import lib.requests' — this is WRONG. The correct import is just 'import requests'. Copy requests.py to /lib/ and import by name.

rules.title: 'micropython' vs 'esp' vs 'esp32' modules
rules.explanation: Three different modules with different scopes:

modules.name: micropython
modules.key_funcs: const(), opt_level(), mem_info(), alloc_emergency_exception_buf(), schedule(), kbd_intr()

modules.name: esp
modules.key_funcs: osdebug(), sleep_type(), flash_size(), flash_read(), flash_write(), flash_erase()

modules.name: esp32
modules.key_funcs: wake_on_touch(), NVS(), Partition(), RMT(), raw_temperature(), idf_heap_info()

rules.title: MicroPython uses 2000-01-01 epoch, NOT 1970
rules.explanation: time.time() returns seconds since 2000-01-01 00:00:00 UTC, not 1970-01-01 like CPython.
rules.example:
# ESP32 NTP time setting
import ntptime
ntptime.host = 'pool.ntp.org'
ntptime.settime()  # sets RTC to NTP time

rules.title: No os.environ or environment variables
rules.explanation: ESP32 has no OS environment. Configurations must use files (JSON, ini) or NVS (esp32.NVS).
rules.correct:
import json
with open('/config.json') as f:
    config = json.load(f)
api_key = config['API_KEY']

rules.title: os.listdir() positional arg (NO keyword)
rules.explanation: os.listdir() takes a POSITIONAL argument, not keyword. os.listdir(dir='/') raises TypeError.

rules.title: Wi-Fi blocks; use non-blocking pattern
rules.explanation: wlan.connect() blocks until connected or failed. For interactive apps, poll status in a loop.
rules.non_blocking:
wlan.connect('ssid', 'password')
while not wlan.isconnected():
    time.sleep_ms(100)
    # can do other things here

rules.title: ESP32 has TWO I2C but naming difference
rules.explanation: machine.I2C(0, ...) uses hardware I2C0, machine.I2C(1, ...) uses hardware I2C1. machine.SoftI2C(...) works on any pins.

rules.title: Timer callback takes exactly 1 argument
rules.explanation: The callback function passed to Timer.init() receives the Timer object as its only argument.

rules.title: Const() for compile-time constants
rules.explanation: micropython.const(val) creates compile-time constants (saves RAM). Use for pin numbers, constants.
rules.example:
from micropython import const
LED_PIN = const(2)
SDA_PIN = const(21)
SCL_PIN = const(22)
